project_overview.project_name: M&E Support for NFWP (Nigeria for Women Project)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To provide overall monitoring and evaluation support to NFWP to improve the quality of M&E and the Project Management Information System (PMIS)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Direct beneficiaries are NFWP implementing staff and agencies, particularly the Federal Project Coordination Unit (FPCU) and State Project Coordination Units (SPCUs), as well as the Federal Ministry of Women Affairs and their State counterparts in participating NFWP States. Indirect beneficiaries are women in targeted communities across Nigeria participating in the NFWP program

primary_locations: Additional states expected to join through NFWP Scale Up (up to 14 states total)
geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on participating states
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Document notes heightened insecurity in Nigeria, particularly in the northeast with Boko Haram conflict affecting women and children. Security Risk Management plan referenced for NFWP activities. Conflict-related GBV and Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (SEA) noted as higher in the North

key_components.component_name: Component 1: M&E System Development and Support
key_components.description: Review and revision of NFWP M&E framework, indicators, and PMIS system to identify gaps and areas for improvement. Development of revised M&E system integrating into PMIS and other digital solutions. Includes review of project documents, improvement of M&E strategy and framework, development of additional M&E instruments and reporting templates, and review/revision of PMIS with recommendations for digital solutions

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Iterative Beneficiary Monitoring and Responsive Feedback Mechanism
key_components.description: Development, implementation, and handover of improved Iterative Beneficiary Monitoring (IBM) system and Responsive Feedback Mechanism (RFM). IBM involves quarterly phone-based surveys of WAG members. RFM provides actionable insights to implementers through regular monthly and quarterly responsive feedback sessions. Includes training, data collection system establishment, dashboard development, and technical assistance to NFWP teams

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Capacity Building, Knowledge Management, Sharing and Learning
key_components.description: Building capacity of NFWP M&E and PMIS teams at FPCU and SPCUs on M&E best practices, tools, and methodologies. Training on data collection tools and frameworks for quantitative and qualitative data. Development of knowledge products including progress reports, thematic studies, and case studies. Organization of regular learning events and forums at State, national, regional, and international levels

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Diadem Consult Ltd., a global development organization with over 10 years of experience in project monitoring and evaluation. Selected through competitive call for proposals in September 2021 evaluated by World Bank, Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation, NFWP, and Federal Ministry of Women Affairs
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Diadem will establish a Project implementation team with hub-spoke staffing arrangement in clusters of States. Team includes Regional Research, Monitoring and Evaluation (RME) Advisors, Research Specialist, Management Information System (MIS) Specialist, and Director of Research, Monitoring and Evaluation. Integration into NFWP's ongoing M&E and PMIS reporting systems through weekly, monthly, and quarterly review meetings. Coordination with impact evaluation management firm to ensure monitoring data captures key impact-related measures
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised November 2017, August 2018, November 2020). Diadem maintains pre-qualified vendor list. Procurements above N20,000,000 and specialized procurements published on professional platforms and print media. Diadem to adopt World Bank Standard Procurement Documents (SPD) where necessary. Procurement risk rated Moderate with mitigation measures including training on new Procurement Framework and STEP system
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Diadem and World Bank Task Team will monitor PDO and Intermediate level indicators through twice yearly Progress Reports, monthly meetings on M&E and PMIS with NFWP, and formal review as part of every NFWP Implementation Support Mission. Diadem has robust internal M&E systems utilizing electronic format. Full M&E plan and monitoring tools to be outlined in Project Implementation Manual. Director of MEL will oversee RME Advisors and research/data collection teams. M&E arrangements include weekly, monthly, and quarterly review meetings integrated into NFWP's systems

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks to personal and project data arising from poor data access and management procedures as they relate to the M&E system of NFWP and the MIS of NFWP
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Robust data collection, retention, and protection policies to be developed with NFWP

identified_risks.risk_description: Governance or political issues which may affect delivery of NFWP, such as falling political will as election period approaches. Document notes upcoming February 2023 general elections creating uncertainty on policy direction
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: NFWP will ensure that Women Affinity Groups (WAGs) are not coopted for political purposes and that NFWP structures remain operational throughout elections and into a new administration

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential spread of COVID-19 during implementation of capacity building activities, training sessions, and public engagements at Federal, State, LGA, and Ward levels
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Diadem to implement measures ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements stipulated by Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC), WHO, and World Bank. Stakeholders Engagement Plan (SEP) includes guidelines for conducting meetings under COVID-19 constraints including social distancing, use of masks, hand washing and sanitizing

identified_risks.risk_category: Environmental and Social - Sexual Exploitation and Abuse/Sexual Harassment
identified_risks.risk_description: Project-induced sexual exploitation and abuse/sexual harassment (SEA/SH) issues potentially perpetrated by contracted Trainers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Labour Management Plan (LMP) prepared by Plan International includes Codes of Conduct for relevant project contractors and workers addressing SEA/SH behavior and sanctions for infractions. NFWP has pre-existing mechanisms for safe and ethical handling of SEA/SH allegations. GBV Mapping of Service Providers completed in participating states providing database of GBV Service Providers

identified_risks.risk_description: Need to ensure safety and security of workers and project beneficiaries during training due to insecurity situation in Nigeria, especially in participating states. Document notes heightened insecurity and conflict in northeast with Boko Haram
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Diadem will rely on security information provided under NFWP whilst organizing training. NFWP has developed a Security Risk Management plan which will support activities of this project

identified_risks.risk_description: NFWP PCUs have had continued challenges in accurately reporting M&E data. Persistent lack of capacity among Ward Facilitators to undertake complex data entry. Large backlog of data has developed despite multiple capacity building attempts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: This entire project is designed to address these capacity gaps through intensive M&E system overhaul, capacity building, and ongoing mentorship support from Diadem's RME Advisors

identified_risks.risk_description: Diadem lacks experience with new World Bank Procurement Framework and understanding of Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) preparation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: World Bank will provide hands-on support and procurement capacity building during implementation support missions. Training on use of Systematic Tracking of Exchanges in Procurement (STEP). Task Team will develop specific measures to enhance contract management practices including list of procurement and contract execution 'red flags'

identified_risks.risk_description: Overall FM risk of implementing agency Diadem Consult Ltd
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Financial Management Assessment conducted November 2021 rated residual FM risk as Low. Arrangements include: annual workplans and budgets submitted to Bank 2 months before fiscal year; accrual basis accounting with IFRS standards; QuickBooks software for transactions; semi-annual Interim Financial Reports; adequate internal control and FM team; project internal auditor hired for risk-based audits; segregated designated account in USD and Naira; semi-annual FM supervision with annual field supervision

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Low - Technical assistance project with no civil works or land acquisition
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1 - Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS10 - Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - NFWP has pre-existing robust GRM consisting of Federal and State level hotlines and in-person complaint channels through Ward Facilitators and LGA, State, and Federal staff. Built into NFWP PMIS and automatically tracks complaints from receipt through resolution. Diadem will be connected with existing NFWP GRM. Diadem's policies and procedures for grievance redress will be aligned with NFWP and agreed with Bank and NFWP FPCU. Labour Management Plan includes GRM specifically for project workers to raise issues
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project directly supports Nigeria for Women Project (NFWP) focused on women's economic empowerment. Document extensively discusses gender gaps in Nigeria: 70% of women live below poverty line; maternal mortality ratio 576 per 100,000; female school enrollment lags boys; 2/3 of 10.5 million out-of-school children are girls. Gender-based violence prevalent with 31% of women aged 15-49 experiencing physical or sexual violence. Project supports Women Affinity Groups (WAGs) as service delivery platforms. Gender inequality identified as constraining factor to economic growth
vulnerable_groups: Women and girls in Nigeria, particularly in rural areas
Women in northern Nigeria facing child marriage, wife beating, restricted movement
Women in southern Nigeria facing female genital mutilation, intimate partner violence, trafficking
Women affected by conflict in northeast (Boko Haram violence, abduction, forced marriages)
Women in poverty (70% of women live below poverty line)
Small-scale women producers in agricultural commodity value chains
Women with limited access to finance (only 35% have bank accounts vs 55% of men)
Women in rural areas with limited access to financial services

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses conflict-related risks through: (1) Security Risk Management plan referenced for NFWP activities; (2) Diadem to rely on security information provided under NFWP when organizing training; (3) Recognition that conflict in northeast has contributed to rise in targeted violence against women and children by Boko Haram including abduction and violence; (4) Women increasingly used as instruments of war including suicide bombing and forced marriages, making them vulnerable to stigmatization; (5) Project supports women's economic empowerment as pathway to address gender inequality exacerbated by conflict; (6) GBV Mapping of Service Providers completed to support safe handling of conflict-related GBV cases
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria faces multiple fragility factors: (1) Heightened insecurity noted as threat to economic outlook; (2) Conflict in northeast with Boko Haram affecting women and children; (3) Economic stagnation since 2015 with growth reduced to 1.1% average 2015-2021 due to oil price decline, insecurity, policy reversals; (4) Poverty deepening with 80 million people living in poverty and 12 million pushed into poverty by inflation since 2020; (5) Poor human capital outcomes with Nigeria 168th out of 174 countries on Human Capital Index; (6) Weak institutions and service delivery; (7) Upcoming February 2023 elections creating policy uncertainty; (8) Gender inequality and discriminatory social norms limiting women's economic participation; (9) Structural market failures and limited access to finance constraining economic growth
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Document notes: (1) Heightened insecurity in Nigeria as threat to growth outlook; (2) Conflict in northeast with Boko Haram; (3) Women increasingly targeted for violence, abduction, forced marriages, suicide bombing; (4) Need to ensure safety and security of workers and project beneficiaries during training; (5) Diadem will rely on NFWP's Security Risk Management plan when organizing training activities; (6) Project activities will be organized considering security constraints in participating states
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No explicit mention of displacement or refugee populations in the PAD. However, document notes conflict in northeast has contributed to rise in targeted violence against women and children by Boko Haram including abduction, suggesting potential displacement issues, though not explicitly addressed in this M&E support project
